what: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: Shiny app reviewing validating ecological data extracted ecoextract package.

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: ecoreview part three-package workflow extracting validating structured data scientific literature: ohseer - OCR processing extract text PDFs ecoextract - LLM-powered structured data extraction OCR text ecoreview - Human---loop review validation interface (package) ecoextract processes documents, use ecoreview review, edit, validate extracted records side--side document viewer.

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
code: # renv (recommended for renv-managed projects) renv::install("n8layman/ecoreview")  # pak pak::pak("n8layman/ecoreview")  # remotes remotes::install_github("n8layman/ecoreview")  # devtools devtools::install_github("n8layman/ecoreview")

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
code: library(ecoreview)  # Launch with defaults (looks for ecoextract_records.db in current directory) run_app()

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
code: library(ecoreview)  # Launch with custom configuration run_app(   title = "ChiroScan: Bat Interaction Review",   app_name = "ChiroScan",   github_url = "https://github.com/n8layman/bat-interactions",   export_prefix = "bat_interactions" )

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: Use priority_cols pin important columns left, visible_cols show columns reviewers need. Neither parameter affects underlying database — display-settings. Reviewers can adjust column order visibility time using Columns button table, opens drag--drop panel Visible Hidden zones.
code: run_app(   title = "ChiroScan: Bat Interaction Review",   # Show these columns first (left-most), in this order   priority_cols = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type"),   # Show only these columns on load (others hidden but still editable via Columns button)   visible_cols  = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type",                     "Host_Species", "Pathogen_Species") )

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: Side--side document review: View OCR text original PDF alongside extracted records Inline cell editing: Edit extracted data directly table change tracking Column management: Reorder show/hide columns via drag--drop panel — database never modified Evidence highlighting: Click record highlight supporting text document Audit trail: Full history edits made review Accuracy metrics: Precision, recall, F1 scores calculated verified documents Document navigation: Toggle dropdown filename document ID display jump directly known ID Export Records CSV: Downloads non-deleted records joined document metadata, wide columns (OCR text, reasoning) stripped readability Export Documents CSV: Downloads summary documents — ID, filename, author, year, title, pipeline statuses (OCR, metadata, extraction, refinement) Save Database: Download current SQLite file edits applied Schema migration warning: Alerts connect database uses pre-UUID schema requires migration

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: R >= 4.0 ecoextract package (database output)

what: Getting Started with the Full Workflow
title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: ’re new ecosystem, start ecoextract: See ecoextract documentation complete workflow details, including API setup, custom schemas, parallel processing.
code: # 1. Install the extraction packages pak::pak("n8layman/ohseer") pak::pak("n8layman/ecoextract")  # 2. Process your PDFs library(ecoextract) results <- process_documents(   pdf_path = "path/to/pdfs/",   db_conn = "ecoextract_records.db" )  # 3. Review and validate the results renv::install("n8layman/ecoreview") library(ecoreview) run_app(db_path = "ecoextract_records.db")

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: ecoreview::split_db() divides database n roughly-equal part files multiple reviewers can work subset independently. ecoreview::combine_db() merges finished parts back single database, accepting either directory path (auto-discovers *_part_N.db files) explicit vector paths. writing data, combine_db() validates parts share schema errors clear message differ, preventing silent data corruption mismatched versions. Opening combined database run_app() gives full accuracy metrics across reviewers — verified documents, field-level edits, deletion flags preserved exactly, accuracy modal reflects complete picture review.
code: # Split into 4 sequential parts (lowest document IDs first) — written next to the source file parts <- ecoreview::split_db("ecoextract_records.db", n = 4) # → ecoextract_records_part_1.db ... ecoextract_records_part_4.db  # Random assignment instead of sequential parts <- ecoreview::split_db("ecoextract_records.db", n = 4, random = TRUE, seed = 42)  # Each reviewer launches the app pointed at their own part ecoreview::run_app(db_path = "ecoextract_records_part_2.db")  # Once all reviewers are done, recombine (supply directory or explicit paths) ecoreview::combine_db(   "path/to/parts/",   output_path = "ecoextract_records_reviewed.db" )

what: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: Merges multiple part databases (created split_db()) one, verifying parts share identical schema proceeding.

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
code: combine_db(   db_paths,   output_path,   overwrite = FALSE,   pattern = "_part_\\d+\\.db$" )

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: db_paths character vector >= 2 part database file paths, single directory path. directory supplied, pattern used discover part files within . output_path Path combined output database. Must already exist unless overwrite = TRUE. overwrite TRUE, delete output_path combining. Default FALSE. pattern Regular expression used filter files db_paths directory. Defaults "_part_\\d+\\.db$", matches naming convention produced split_db().

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: Path combined database (invisibly).

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: db_paths can supplied two ways: character vector file paths (existing behaviour). single directory path — files directory whose names match pattern collected automatically sorted merging. Schema check: table names, column names, column types must match across parts. Mismatched tables columns raise error; mismatched column types raise warning (SQLite's flexible typing often makes harmless, investigate). Overlap check: document IDs expected disjoint across parts. overlap reported warning; overlapping rows still inserted (duplicate skipped INSERT IGNORE). Primary-key remapping: tables carry auto-increment integer primary key reference interaction_id (e.g. human_edits) inserted without original PK SQLite assigns new non-conflicting values. foreign-key relationships preserved interaction IDs unique across parts.

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Supply a directory — part files discovered automatically combine_db("review_parts/", output_path = "extractions_reviewed.db")  # Supply paths explicitly parts <- list.files("review_parts", pattern = "_part_\\d+\\.db$",                     full.names = TRUE) combine_db(parts, output_path = "extractions_reviewed.db") } # }

what: Find a matching span in plain text for an evidence fragment — find_best_match_in_html
title: Find a matching span in plain text for an evidence fragment — find_best_match_in_html
text: Splits evidence alphanumeric tokens (words/numbers) separated non-alphanumeric runs.  tokens, [^-Za-z0-9]+ used punctuation differences ("Table 1: ..." vs "Table 1 ...") ignored. Within token, [^-Za-z0-9]* inserted every pair adjacent characters OCR line-break hyphens ("amphis-tome") match evidence word ("amphistome").  Search case-insensitive.

text: Launches interactive Shiny interface reviewing validating ecological data extracted ecoextract.

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
code: run_app(   title = "EcoReview: Data Validation",   app_name = "EcoReview",   github_url = "https://github.com/n8layman/ecoreview",   export_prefix = "ecoextract",   db_path = NULL,   priority_cols = NULL,   visible_cols = NULL,   pdf_dir = NULL,   ... )

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
text: title Application title displayed browser tab header app_name Short name application (used export filenames) github_url Optional GitHub repository URL source link. Set NULL hide. export_prefix Prefix exported CSV filenames (default: "ecoextract") db_path Optional path SQLite database file connect startup. supplied file exists, app opens immediately without showing connect modal. NULL (default) shows connect modal. priority_cols Character vector column names display first (left-). Columns present data silently ignored. columns follow original order. NULL (default) uses data frame column order. visible_cols Character vector column names show. columns hidden table display (underlying data unchanged). NULL (default) shows columns. pdf_dir Optional path directory containing PDF files. Use .db file stored separately PDFs app launched different working directory. path stored database resolve, app falls back looking file's basename . NULL (default) uses path stored database. ... Additional arguments passed shiny::runApp()

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
text: Launches Shiny application (return)

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run with defaults run_app()  # Customize for a specific project with priority and visible columns run_app(   title = "ChiroScan: Bat Interaction Review",   app_name = "ChiroScan",   github_url = "https://github.com/n8layman/bat-interactions",   export_prefix = "bat_interactions",   priority_cols = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type"),   visible_cols  = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type",                     "Host_Species", "Pathogen_Species") ) } # }

what: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: Distributes documents evenly across n part databases multiple reviewers can work independently. part receives full schema plus subset documents (associated records audit trail entries) assigned part.

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
code: split_db(   db_path,   n,   output_dir = NULL,   prefix = NULL,   random = FALSE,   seed = NULL )

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: db_path Path source SQLite database. n Integer >= 2. Number parts create. output_dir Directory output part files. Defaults directory db_path. prefix Filename prefix part files. Defaults source filename (without extension). Parts named {prefix}_part_01.db, {prefix}_part_02.db, etc. random Logical. FALSE (default), documents assigned ascending document_id order part gets consecutive block IDs. TRUE, documents shuffled randomly assignment. seed Optional integer seed reproducible random assignment random = TRUE. Ignored random = FALSE.

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: Character vector paths created part databases (invisibly).

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: reviewers finish, recombine combine_db().

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Sequential split (default): part 1 gets lowest IDs, part 2 next block, etc. parts <- split_db("extractions.db", n = 4)  # Random split with a reproducible seed parts <- split_db("extractions.db", n = 4, random = TRUE, seed = 42,                   output_dir = "review_parts") } # }

what: Standardize names across benchmark replicates — standardize_benchmark_names
title: Standardize names across benchmark replicates — standardize_benchmark_names
text: Reads benchmark_records.csv, pools unique names specified columns, sends LLM standardization, adds standardized columns CSV. Name mappings cached name_mappings_{label}.csv avoid re-running LLM subsequent calls.

title: Standardize names across benchmark replicates — standardize_benchmark_names
code: standardize_benchmark_names(   benchmark_dir,   name_columns = list(list(column = "Host_Name", type = "host", std_name =     "Host_Name_std"), list(column = "Pathogen_Name", type = "pathogen", std_name =     "Pathogen_Name_std")),   model = NULL )

title: Standardize names across benchmark replicates — standardize_benchmark_names
text: benchmark_dir Directory containing benchmark_records.csv name_columns list lists, : column Column name CSV standardize type Name type LLM prompt ("host" "pathogen") std_name Name new standardized column model LLM model use standardization (default: Claude via ellmer)

title: Standardize names across benchmark replicates — standardize_benchmark_names
text: updated records tibble (also overwrites benchmark_records.csv)

title: Standardize names across benchmark replicates — standardize_benchmark_names

what: Standardize a vector of names using an LLM — standardize_name_vector
title: Standardize a vector of names using an LLM — standardize_name_vector
text: Sends unique names LLM batches taxonomic standardization. Resolves common names, misspellings, outdated nomenclature, parenthetical qualifiers canonical scientific names.
